evidence: Acerbate strongly resonates with intensifying bitterness (material layer, essential resonance), acting as a destructive force (symmetry) and causing fundamental transformation (temporal). It is deeply tied to sensory perception and lived experience (epistemological).

Acerbity strongly resonates with material existence (taste), destructive symmetry (as opposed to deliciousness), and essential resonance (core bitterness). It is perceived sensorily and experienced directly, with predictable manifestations in individual contexts. Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic or existential depth beyond sensory and relational opposition.

Acrid strongly resonates with sensory perception (direct taste/smell), lived experience, and material reality. Its essential resonance stems from being a core sensory quality. The destructive symmetry reflects its unpleasantness, while predictability aligns with expected negative reactions. Scores for identification and semantic value reflect categorization and meaning tied to bitterness.

Acridity strongly resonates with individual sensory experience (scale.individual, epistemological.perception/experience), its essential bitter quality (resonance.essential, ontological.semantic/material), and its opposition to PER.delicious (symmetry.destructive). Scores reflect its direct, predictable manifestation in taste perception and transient temporal influence.

Acridness strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception), material existence (ontological.material), and its destructive quality (symmetry.destructive). It is essential to the core experience of bitterness (resonance.essential) and tied to contingent conditions (lawfulness.contingent). Lower scores reflect its individual, transient, and localized nature.

Bitterness strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception 9), material reality (ontological.material 9), and its essential core (resonance.essential 8). It operates at an individual scale (scale.individual 8) and relates to destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive 7) as the inverse of deliciousness. Scores drop where connections are indirect or speculative.

Bitterness strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception) and material existence (ontological.material). Its destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive) contrasts with deliciousness. It operates at individual scale (scale.individual) and connects catalytically (interconnection.catalytic) when influencing behavior. Persistence (temporal.continuation) and predictability (lawfulness.predictable) align with its stable, sensory nature.

The highest scores reflect direct sensory perception (10), material basis (8), and its negative valence (destructive:7). Bitterness is tied to individual experience (9) and aesthetic balance (3). Lower scores reflect its transient influence over time and limited systemic impact.

Bitterness strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological), individual experience (scale), material basis (ontological), and its destructive opposition to deliciousness (symmetry). It is predictable (lawfulness) and essential (resonance) to taste. Minor aesthetic value exists in balanced contexts.

Harshness in taste resonates strongly with individual sensory experience (scale.individual=10, epistemological.perception=10, epistemological.experience=10). Its material basis (ontological.material=10) and opposition to PER.delicious (symmetry.destructive=10) are core. Secondary scores reflect its contingent nature (lawfulness.contingent=2), potential to trigger reactions (interconnection.catalytic=2), and negative aesthetic valence (value_generation.aesthetic=2).

Harshness strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception 10) and material experience (ontological.material 9). Its bitter taste is an individual sensory quality (scale.individual 8) and aligns with destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive 9). It has essential resonance (resonance.essential 8) as it touches the core of unpleasant taste, contrasting its symmetric pair PER.delicious.

Pungency strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception 10) and material existence (ontological.material 10), as it directly relates to the sharp, physical taste of bitterness. Its destructive symmetry (8) aligns with the unpleasant aspect of PER.bitter, contrasting with PER.delicious. Scores for catalytic interconnection (5) reflect how pungency can trigger strong reactions, while essential resonance (10) underscores its core role in defining bitterness.

Pungent strongly resonates with sensory perception (10) and material existence (10) as a sharp taste. It aligns with individual experience (10) and destructive symmetry (8) as an unpleasant counterpart to deliciousness. Moderate scores reflect its catalytic effects (4), essential sensory resonance (7), and aesthetic valence (5).

The highest scores reflect 'rough' as a direct sensory experience (epistemological.perception/experience), tied to physical taste (ontological.material). Its destructive nature (symmetry.destructive) and core resonance (resonance.essential) align with bitterness's unpleasantness. Predictable lawfulness (lawfulness.predictable) and contingent dependency on context (lawfulness.contingent) also strongly resonate.

Roughness in the context of bitterness (PER.bitter) strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception 5), material existence (ontological.material 5), and its destructive nature (symmetry.destructive 5). It also connects to essential resonance (resonance.essential 4) as bitterness touches the core of sensory experience. The catalytic effect (interconnection.catalytic 3) reflects how bitterness triggers reactions, while its superficial manifestation (resonance.superficial 3) aligns with direct sensory engagement.

The word 'unpleasant' strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception), individual experience (scale.individual), and material existence (ontological.material). Its opposition to 'delicious' (symmetry.destructive) and its role in predictable taste experiences (lawfulness.predictable) further amplify its resonance. The semantic layer (ontological.semantic) and aesthetic judgment (value_generation.aesthetic) reflect its evaluative nature.
